content.text: RBAC is in place. Now I'll deploy the headless service, public service, StatefulSet, and PodDisruptionBudget.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - weight: 100
            podAffinityTerm:
              labelSelector:
                matchLabels:
                  app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
                  app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
              topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        ports:
        - name: grpc
          containerPort: 26257
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-host=$(hostname).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --cache=25% \
            --max-sql-memory=25%
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Deploy CockroachDB services, PDB, and StatefulSet

content.text: All resources created. Now I'll wait for pods to come up and then run `cockroach init` to bootstrap the cluster.

input.command:
echo "Waiting for all 3 pods to be Running..."
for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  RUNNING=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)
  TOTAL=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
  echo "  [${i}] ${RUNNING}/${TOTAL} Running"
  if [ "$RUNNING" -eq 3 ] && [ "$TOTAL" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods are Running."
    break
  fi
  sleep 5
done
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb

content.thinking:
The pods are starting but not all are ready yet. The readiness probe is not passing. Let me check more carefully. Only crdb-cluster-0 seems to exist and it's `0/1 Running` (not ready). This is because the cluster needs to be initialized first with `cockroach init`.

Let me wait for pods to be available and then run `cockroach init`.

Actually, looking at the output: `RUNNING=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cockroachdb --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)` — the grep is looking for "Running" in status, but the pods are `0/1 Running` (not Ready). The pod starts but readiness probe fails until cluster is initialized.
I see the issue now — the loop was counting only one pod existing at a time (crdb-cluster-0), and the grep matched "Running" in the status field, but `0/1` means it's not actually ready. My condition waiting for three ready pods was never going to be satisfied because only one pod was running and it wasn't passing its readiness check.

content.text: The readiness probes are failing until the cluster is initialized. Let me check pod status and run `cockroach init`.

content.thinking:
Only crdb-cluster-0 is running. The StatefulSet starts pods one by one; it won't start crdb-cluster-1 until crdb-cluster-0 is ready. And crdb-cluster-0 won't be ready until the cluster is initialized. But to initialize the cluster, at least one pod needs to be contactable.

Actually for CockroachDB, the pods join each other via the `--join` flag. In insecure mode, the pods should start and wait to be initialized. The readiness probe checks `/health?ready=1` which will fail until the cluster is formed.

The issue is that the StatefulSet won't roll out pods 1 and 2 until pod 0 is ready, but pod 0 won't be ready until we run `cockroach init`. This is a chicken-and-egg problem.
I need to break this deadlock by running the initialization command directly against the first pod, even though it's not passing readiness checks yet. Let me exec into crdb-cluster-0 and initialize the cluster in insecure mode.

content.text: The StatefulSet waits for each pod to be ready before starting the next — I need to initialize the cluster now so pod 0 becomes ready and pods 1 & 2 can start.
